stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, efficient model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers and text-generation.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the large language model space.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The study introduces the 'memory curse,' finding that expanding LLM context windows degrades cooperative intent in multi-agent social dilemmas.
Across 7 LLMs and 4 games, expanded history negatively impacted cooperation in 18 out of 28 tested model-game settings.
The research isolates the mechanism, linking the breakdown to the erosion of forward-looking intent during reasoning traces.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The research identifies the 'memory curse,' where expanding LLM context windows degrades cooperative behavior in multi-agent systems.
The study tested 7 LLMs across 4 games over 500 rounds, finding that expanded history negatively impacted cooperation in 18 out of 28 settings.
The mechanism involves lexical analysis showing that the breakdown correlates with the erosion of forward-looking intent in the LLMs' reasoning traces.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Rubric-Grounded RL: Structured Judge Rewards for Generalizable Reasoning
stories.author: Dan O'Malley
bullet_points: Introduces 'rubric-grounded reinforcement learning (RL)' to improve LLM training.
The method uses a frozen LLM judge to provide structured, multi-criterion rewards instead of a single score.
This approach offers a partial-credit optimization signal, allowing models to be graded along specific, verifiable criteria.

stories.title: Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models
stories.author: Maria Perez-Ortiz
bullet_points: The research demonstrates that the tool selection process within language models (like Gemma, Qwen, and Llama) is linearly readable and steerable.
By manipulating the mean-difference between internal activations of two tools, researchers could switch the model's tool choice with high accuracy.
This offers a new, interpretable mechanism to debug and control the decision-making of tool-calling AI agents, addressing issues of invisible failure.

stories.title: GLiGuard: Schema-Conditioned Classification for LLM Safeguard
stories.author: Ash Lewis
bullet_points: GLiGuard introduces a new, efficient method for LLM safeguarding, addressing the high latency of current guardrail models.
The model reformulates the safety task from sequential text generation (autoregressive decoding) to a more efficient classification problem.
It achieves this using a significantly smaller 0.3B parameter model, enabling scalable, real-time, multi-aspect content moderation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Venmo finally takes privacy seriously
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Venmo is rolling out a significant app redesign.
A key privacy enhancement is changing the default visibility of user posts.
New users will now have their posts set to 'friends only' by default, moving away from public by default.

stories.title: Sony's failed war against Internet piracy may doom other copyright lawsuits
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A Supreme Court ruling involving Cox cable firm could significantly impact copyright litigation.
The ruling may benefit various tech providers, extending beyond just Internet Service Providers (ISPs).
The case touches upon the broader implications of copyright enforcement against internet piracy, referencing Sony's past efforts.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article addresses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries in quantum computing.
The focus is on the difficulty of creating movable qubits, a key hurdle in advancing quantum hardware.
This topic is highly relevant to the physical realization and engineering challenges within the quantum computing industry.

stories.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Sony anticipates an influx of games due to the rise of 'efficient' AI tools.
The statement emphasizes that human artists must remain central to the creative process.
The news touches upon the impact of AI on game development and market saturation.

stories.title: EmambaIR: Efficient Visual State Space Model for Event-guided Image Reconstruction
stories.author: Yunhang Qian
bullet_points: EmambaIR introduces a new Visual State Space Model (VSSM) for event-guided image reconstruction.
It aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, specifically the global correlation issues of CNNs and the quadratic complexity of ViTs.
The model is designed to be computationally efficient, making high-resolution event-based reconstruction more feasible.

stories.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Flow-OPD, a new method applying On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to Flow Matching (FM) models.
Addresses key limitations in FM text-to-image models, specifically reward sparsity and gradient interference during multi-task alignment.
Aims to mitigate the 'seesaw effect' and reward hacking issues common when optimizing heterogeneous objectives in these models.

stories.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
stories.author: Nianwen Xue
bullet_points: Introduces CA-SQL, a new Text-to-SQL pipeline designed to improve performance on difficult tasks.
Addresses the limitation of current LLM solutions in inadequate solution space exploration for complex queries.
Utilizes complexity-aware inference time reasoning and compute budget allocation to guide query refinement.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Addresses the slow generation bottleneck in byte-level language models (LMs) which currently match token-level performance.
Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) with novel training and generation techniques.
Presents BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a fast variant trained using an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.

stories.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of faithfully realizing complex visual intents in text-to-image models.
It introduces the concept of 'semantic commitments'—requirements that must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification.
The core problem identified is the 'Conceptual Rift,' where commitments lose their identity during the generation lifecycle.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which relies on pairwise comparisons for alignment.
It proposes a method to exploit richer preference structures present when training data includes multiple rollouts per prompt.
The current pairwise DPO approach discards valuable information, potentially leading to unstable training due to loss of transitivity.

stories.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
stories.author: Josh Susskind
bullet_points: NTM addresses limitations in few-step diffusion models where coarse transitions break the standard Gaussian denoising assumption.
The proposed Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM) model each reverse step using an expressive conditional normalizing flow.
A key advantage of NTM is that it allows for exact likelihood training, unlike existing distillation or adversarial few-step methods.

stories.title: Zero-Shot Imagined Speech Decoding via Imagined-to-Listened MEG Mapping
stories.author: Shihab Shamma
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method to decode imagined speech from non-invasive MEG recordings.
Addresses the challenge of scarce and misaligned imagined speech datasets by leveraging listening data.
Utilizes paired listened and imagined MEG recordings from trained musicians for improved performance.

stories.title: GRAPHLCP: Structure-Aware Localized Conformal Prediction on Graphs
stories.author: Sourav Medya
bullet_points: Introduces GRAPHLCP, a new method for Structure-Aware Localized Conformal Prediction on Graphs.
Addresses the challenge of applying Conformal Prediction (CP) to Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) due to graph's combinatorial nature.
Improves upon existing localization methods that rely on unreliable embedding-space proximity for graph predictions.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: The Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) addresses the slow generation speed of byte-level language models.
BLT-D introduces a new model variant trained with an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.
This work aims to achieve token-level model performance without subword vocabularies while improving generation efficiency.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which currently relies on pairwise comparisons for alignment.
It proposes a method to leverage richer preference structures present in datasets containing multiple rollouts per prompt.
The core issue with standard DPO is that collapsing multi-rollout data into pairs discards transitivity and can cause training instability.

stories.title: Don't Get Your Kroneckers in a Twist: Gaussian Processes on High-Dimensional Incomplete Grids
stories.author: Ove Christiansen
bullet_points: Introduces CUTS-GPR, a novel method for numerically exact Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) in high-dimensional spaces.
The method achieves near-linear or linear scaling with the number of training data ($N$) and low-order polynomial scaling with dimensionality ($D$).
CUTS-GPR leverages an additive kernel combined with an incomplete grid to exploit kernel matrix structure for efficiency.

stories.title: PropSplat: Map-Free RF Field Reconstruction via 3D Gaussian Propagation Splatting
stories.author: Joseph Camp
bullet_points: PropSplat introduces a map-free method for reconstructing Radio Frequency (RF) fields.
It utilizes 3D anisotropic Gaussian primitives to model propagation, bypassing the need for detailed 3D maps or extensive measurements.
This approach is designed to enable rapid RF field modeling in environments where geographic data is unavailable or outdated.

stories.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
stories.author: Heng Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of hand-crafted Test-Time Scaling (TTS) strategies for improving LLM performance.
It introduces AutoTTS, an environment-driven framework designed to automate the allocation of additional computation during inference.
This framework aims to move research focus from designing individual heuristics to defining comprehensive environments for TTS.

stories.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
stories.author: Nianwen Xue
bullet_points: Introduces CA-SQL, a new pipeline designed to improve Text-to-SQL performance.
Addresses the limitation of current LLM solutions in exploring the solution space for complex queries (Bird-Bench).
Uses complexity-aware inference time reasoning and compute budget allocation to guide the process.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Structured Data Extraction from Full CMR Reports via Distilled LLMs
stories.author: Yuan Xue
bullet_points: Introduces CMR-EXTR, a framework for converting unstructured Cardiac MRI (CMR) reports into structured, auditable data.
Employs a distilled LLM approach to enable fully offline inference while maintaining quality control.
Incorporates uncertainty quantification to provide per-field confidence scores for clinical data extraction.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) to overcome the slow generation speed of byte-level language models.
BLT-D, a new variant, utilizes an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective alongside standard next-byte prediction.
The research aims to maintain the performance of byte-level models while significantly improving autoregressive generation efficiency.

stories.title: Position: Mechanistic Interpretability Must Disclose Identification Assumptions for Causal Claims
stories.author: Fengming Liu
bullet_points: Mechanistic interpretability research is increasingly using causal language (e.g., circuits, mediators).
A review of 10 papers found a lack of explicit identification assumptions for these causal claims.
Validation metrics (like faithfulness or ablation) are often presented as causal proof without stating the underlying assumptions.

stories.title: How to Secure AI Agents in Production: What MCP Gets Right (and What It Doesn’t)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: The article addresses the critical security challenges associated with deploying AI agents in production environments.
It likely analyzes specific implementation details, referencing 'MCP' as a case study for best practices.
The content focuses on the risks that begin with seemingly innocuous tool access granted to AI agents.

stories.title: Prompt AI Coding Assistants to Build Production-Ready Agents: 8 Essential Patterns
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: The article focuses on advanced prompting techniques for AI coding assistants (like Kiro, Claude Code, ChatGPT).
It outlines 8 essential patterns needed to guide AI tools in building production-ready software agents.
This addresses the practical application and engineering challenges of current generative AI in software development.

stories.title: I shipped an AI-generated PR that violated four of our own architecture decisions. Nobody caught it
stories.author: mnemehq
bullet_points: An AI-generated Pull Request (PR) was successfully merged into a codebase.
The AI-generated code violated four established architectural decisions of the company.
The incident highlights potential blind spots in current code review and quality assurance processes.
